methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.3B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: TAE Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $6B

private.name: Form Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series G), post-money $3.5B

data.narrative:
Watershed is a Series G climate and energy company founded by Taylor Francis that has raised over $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private and one public firm operating in the same sector at equivalent or adjacent stages, including TAE Technologies, Helion, Form Energy, and Sila Nanotechnologies.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the last post-money valuation of $1.8B and applying a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier to the next funding stage, yields $2.2B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector comparables at Series G or late Stage produced a median valuation of $4.5B, with a range of $3.3B (Sila Nanotechnologies) to $6.0B (TAE Technologies). TAE and Form Energy, both at Series G, valued at $6.0B and $3.5B respectively, bracket the early-stage end of the peer set.

The triangulated range supported by these methods spans $1.9B to $5.1B, with a median of $3.4B. The current $1.8B valuation sits at the low end of this range. Key uncertainties include the degree to which stage-progression multipliers apply uniformly across heterogeneous climate-tech subsectors, and whether the peer set adequately reflects Watershed's specific technological maturity and capital efficiency relative to TAE and Form Energy.
